title: Re-add shell:true for Windows .cmd CLI resolution (spawn ENOENT fix)
root_cause: On Windows/MSYS2, npm global binaries (codex, claude, gemini) are .cmd wrapper files. spawn() without shell:true cannot resolve .cmd files — it needs shell resolution. The prior removal of shell:true (step2f security fix) broke Windows CLI invocation.
fix_summary: Re-added shell:true to all 6 spawn calls across both invoker copies. This is safe because prompt is piped via stdin in every case — never in the args array.

IMP-004.evidence: Fix applied to all 6 spawn calls across both files: shared/llm-invoker/invoker.ts (canonical) and tools/agent-role-builder/src/shared-imports.ts (local copy). Grep for 'shell: true' confirms exactly 6 instances in these 2 files, 3 per file.

IMP-009.evidence: Grep for 'args.push(params.prompt)' returns zero matches in current code. All 6 spawn calls pipe prompt via proc.stdin.write(params.prompt) followed by proc.stdin.end(). shell:true is used only for command resolution (.cmd files on Windows), not for argument processing. No injection vector exists because user input never enters the shell argument string.

verification.prompt_not_in_args: grep 'args.push(params.prompt)' returns 0 matches in source files
verification.shell_true_count: grep 'shell: true' returns exactly 6 matches in the 2 invoker files
verification.tsc_shared: npx tsc --noEmit in shared/ passed with 0 errors
verification.tsc_agent_role_builder: npx tsc --noEmit in tools/agent-role-builder/ passed with 0 errors
